codeReview.summary: The profile service implements full CRUD operations (POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /api/profile) with name validation and encryption at rest. The implementation is well-tested with 59 passing tests across 4 test files. The worker found the code already committed in repo init commit 7bf2c5d and performed verification via typecheck, lint, unit tests, and manual curl checks — all passing. The code follows project conventions: Express routes, TypeScript strict mode, better-sqlite3, AES-256-GCM encryption, and Vitest for testing.

issues.description: ValidationError class is duplicated in routes/profile.ts rather than imported from services/profile.ts. The comment says 'to avoid circular dependency issues' but the profile routes do not import the profile service — they call the DatabaseService directly. There is no actual circular dependency preventing the import of ValidationError from services/profile.js. This should be cleaned up to avoid maintaining two identical classes.

The route handlers bypass the ProfileService entirely and call getDatabase() / db.createProfile() / db.updateProfile() directly. This means input validation (empty name, whitespace trimming) is duplicated in both the routes and the service layer. If a consumer calls the service directly (e.g., for programmatic API), validation works; but the routes have their own copy. Not a bug since both validate, but it's an architecture concern — the routes should use ProfileService as the single source of truth for validation logic.

The development fallback encryption key 'development-key-change-in-production' means any data encrypted in development is protected by a key stored in source control. This is acceptable for development but should be prominently warned about (it is noted as 'change-in-production', which is fine for a non-production app).

The route tests do not test the actual profile routes module (backend/routes/profile.ts). Instead, createTestApp() re-implements the same route handler logic inline. This means if the actual routes/profile.ts handler has a bug but the test helper has the correct logic, the test will pass against incorrect production code. The tests should import and mount the actual router: app.use('/api/profile', profileRouter) with a way to inject the test database's ProfileService.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker found the entire feature already committed as part of the initial repository setup (commit 7bf2c5d). The worker's role was essentially verification — running tests, linting, typechecking, and curl verification. The backend-worker skill appears to have been invoked correctly and followed its procedure. The transcript shows the worker explored the codebase structure, read all relevant files, ran validation, started the server for manual API testing, and produced a complete handoff. No deviations detected.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker read existing backend/services/profile.ts, backend/routes/profile.ts, and all test files — all already present. Worker ran: npm run typecheck (0 errors), npm run lint (0 errors), npm run test:backend (59 tests pass), then manual curl verification against all CRUD endpoints.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The route tests (backend/routes/profile.test.ts) reimplement route handlers inline rather than importing the actual routes module. This is a test architecture pattern that diverges from testing the real production code. Future workers should be aware of this pattern and either fix it or follow it consistently.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/routes/profile.test.ts defines createTestApp() with inline route handlers (lines 23-110) instead of importing router from backend/routes/profile.ts. Compare to backend/services/profile.test.ts which correctly imports ProfileService from the actual module.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No additional services or commands were started beyond what is already documented in services.yaml. The worker used PORT=3100 for the backend server, which aligns with services.yaml. No gaps in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker started backend with PORT=3100 matching services.yaml configuration. No undocumented services launched.

summary: Feature foundation-profile-service is fully implemented and passes review. All 5 CRUD endpoints (POST/GET/PUT/DELETE) are functional with proper HTTP status codes (201/200/204/400/404). Name validation correctly rejects empty/whitespace names with 400. Profile data is encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM. 59 tests pass across all layers. The worker found the code already committed and performed thorough verification. Minor non-blocking issues identified: (1) ValidationError duplicated in routes, (2) routes bypass ProfileService and duplicate validation logic, (3) route tests reimplement handlers inline rather than testing the actual production router. None of these are blocking — they are architectural improvements for future refactoring.
